Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The event logs show 5 cases cases 1 to 5 with different traces We need to represent a partial order that includes all activities some optional like LB some loops for example RT loop around TST and IQC loop around AS

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We have OR MS QS AS maybe IQC AS repeated multiple times then TST maybe RT TST loop then PK maybe LB DP D That appears to be the main flow

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Some cases case 4 has no IQC no RT Case 2 has no RT and also LB skipped Cases 1 3 5 have loops RT and IQC

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This suggests a loop Do IQC then either exit loop and go to TST or do AS rework and then go back to IQC again That fits the star IQC AS pattern

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This matches case 1 they had one IQC then AS then TST Case 3 they had three IQC and two AS then TST Case 5 they had two cycles then TST

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For retesting we have TST first then if fails RT then go back to TST This matches star TST RT Indeed in case 1 TST RT TST in case 5 TST RT TST RT TST

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In case 2 TST is performed once then go to packaging there is no RT That is consistent because the loop may exit after the first TST

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Labeling LB is optional In case 1 3 4 5 LB is present In case 2 LB is skipped

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So we can model this as an XOR choice between LB and a silent skip

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: We need to ensure that the IQC structure handles case 4 because case 4 does not have any IQC they go directly from AS to TST

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If the loop semantics requires at least one execution of the first child then star IQC AS alone is not enough because case 4 skips IQC entirely

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Thus maybe we should model an XOR between a loop and a silent skip

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 can then go from AS to the skip branch and then to TST while cases 1 3 5 can choose the IQC AS loop

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus the overall process is OR then MS then QS then AS then optional IQC AS loop then TST RT loop then PK then optional LB then DP then D

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The overall partial order can use nodes OR MS QS AS xor_iqc loop_test PK xor_lb DP D with edges OR to MS MS to QS QS to AS AS to xor_iqc xor_iqc to loop_test loop_test to PK PK to xor_lb xor_lb to DP DP to D

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 matches by skipping the IQC branch and the LB branch Case 4 matches by skipping IQC and exiting the test loop after first TST Cases 1 3 5 match by taking the loops as needed
